rationale: The round-4 composition-loss hole is closed. Before persisting a current run's applied refutations, panelGate must fail-closed read the existing refutation_pending_entries, union by slot+round, and write the full set; neither a read nor write failure can clear the RC or reach close/merge. Pre-close reconciliation then reads and processes every uncovered entry in that durable union on panel-present, no-panel, and hatch paths. Each entry must receive a durable panel_refuted audit, a tally-verified refutation_discharged audit, or force refusal. TestPanelRefuted_CrossRun_UnionReconcilesAll pins the exact prior counterexample: X@1 survives the B@2 marker, X is discharged, B is satisfied, and any intervening read/write failure leaves the bead in_progress. Fail-closed MergeMetadata also prevents a later write from erasing either audit. I find no residual sequential retry path that loses or overwrites a durable pending obligation.

findings.defect: Cross-retry pending obligation could be discarded by a newer marker write
findings.evidence: Plan step 5(a) now requires read-then-UNION-then-write of existing and current refutation_pending_entries, never replacement; step 5(c), AC11(d), and TestPanelRefuted_CrossRun_UnionReconcilesAll require full-set reconciliation and both X@1 discharge and B@2 satisfaction before close.

findings.defect: Metadata-read failure could fail open during obligation handling
findings.evidence: Steps 5(a), 5(c), and 5(d) make union and reconciliation reads fail-closed; the marker path blocks with the RC unresolved, while reconciliation refuses with closeBeadFn and ex.completeCalled false.

findings.defect: A later metadata write could erase a durable refutation audit
findings.evidence: Step 6 retains fail-closed MergeMetadata behavior and the audit-survival test, preventing read failure from replacing metadata from an empty map.
The plan retains stable first-wins deduplication by exact slot+round within and across matched panels, with deterministic sorting and duplicate coverage.
